mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) directly signals rising consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon. This mechanism is always active because consumer spending is the dominant component of GDP.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Spearman Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the strategy long (quin) based on RSAFS growth fails to outperform the benchmark (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 3.5%, it signals an overheated labor market that will trigger wage inflation and aggressive Fed tightening, leading to negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days. The threshold is set where labor markets become dangerously tight.
failure_condition: In periods when UNRATE is below 3.5%, the average 20-day forward SPX return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) and strong consumer spending (RSAFS) amplifies equity valuations: low discount rates boost present values of cash flows while robust sales confirm earnings growth. The interaction is always active and mutually reinforcing.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with strong demand
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS * RSAFS growth) has an insignificant coefficient (|t| < 1.96) in a predictive regression over 1-year rolling windows, or the combined signal's Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, each increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations. The negative signal is regime-conditional: it only holds when the Fed is actively raising rates, as opposed to a neutral or easing stance.
failure_condition: During tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS rising over consecutive FOMC meetings), the correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and 20-day forward SPX returns is not significantly negative (|r| < 0.10) or the signal is contradicted by positive returns in more than 60% of tightening events.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding factory output and broader economic momentum, which translates into higher corporate profits and positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon. The signal is always active as a leading indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: The annualized growth rate of INDPRO fails to predict 60-day forward SPX returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or the long-short decile spread is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,200 thousand units signals weakness in the housing sector, which historically precedes broader economic contractions. Such a threshold breach predicts negative equity returns over the next month as recession fears mount.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1,200, the average 20-day forward SPX return is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or less than 55% of such instances produce negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) combined with accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) creates a powerful confirmation loop: employment gains boost income, which in turn drives spending and corporate earnings. The interaction between these two demand-side forces generates consistently positive equity returns. This signal is always active and jointly determined.
failure_condition: The combined signal (e.g., product of PAYEMS growth and RSAFS growth) has a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year period, or the interaction coefficient in a predictive regression fails to be significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) has risen more than 25 basis points above its 3-month moving average, it indicates a hawkish surprise that tightens financial conditions and depresses short-term equity returns. The threshold captures unexpected policy tightening events.
failure_condition: After a hawkish surprise trigger, the average 5-day forward SPX return is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or the frequency of negative returns is below 55%.
